title: He Got Struck by Lightning and Walked Away
script: A man got struck by lightning and somehow walked away alive. He was crossing a parking lot when a bolt hit nearby and the current jumped through him, throwing him to the ground. Lightning carries up to a billion volts and is five times hotter than the surface of the sun, yet most people who are struck actually survive, because the charge flashes over the skin instead of stopping the heart. He walked away with burns shaped like branching lines across his back, called a lightning flower. Doctors say the fact that it was raining may have saved him. So why do some people survive a direct hit?
